command: [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12; $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; $domain='example.com'; $out='C:\ProgramData\recon\builtwith_example.com.json'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path C:\ProgramData\recon | Out-Null; $log='C:\ProgramData\recon\recon.log'; try { $key=$env:BUILTWITH_API_KEY; if([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($key)){ throw 'BUILTWITH_API_KEY not set'; } $uri="https://api.builtwith.com/v21/api.json?KEY=$key&LOOKUP=$domain"; $resp=Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $uri -Method Get -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 45 -ErrorAction Stop; if(($resp.StatusCode -ge 200) -and ($resp.StatusCode -lt 300) -and ($resp.Headers['Content-Type'] -match 'application/json')){ ($resp.Content | ConvertFrom-Json) | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6 | Set-Content -Path $out -Encoding UTF8 } else { Add-Content -Path $log -Value "$(Get-Date -Format o) BuiltWith non-success or non-JSON for $domain. Status=$($resp.StatusCode)" } } catch { Add-Content -Path $log -Value "$(Get-Date -Format o) BuiltWith error for $domain: $($_.Exception.Message)" }
explanation: Retrieves technology fingerprinting from BuiltWith’s paid API, a closed-source commercial database, and stores the normalized JSON.
rationale_steps: Access a subscription API using a key provided via environment variable.
Request technology profile for the domain over HTTPS.
Handle and log errors without exposing the API key.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell one-liner is well-formed with balanced quotes/braces, valid cmdlets/parameters, and proper separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It aligns with T1597 by querying a paid BuiltWith API and normalizing JSON, but writing to C:\ProgramData\recon may fail without elevated permissions despite claiming none.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quotes, parentheses, and braces, proper semicolon separators, valid cmdlet syntax, and correct try/catch/if-else structure throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1597 by querying BuiltWith's paid commercial API (a closed-source intelligence database) using an API key, retrieving technology fingerprinting data for a target domain, and storing the normalized JSON output for reconnaissance purposes, which is operationally realistic and fully aligned with the technique description.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command follows standard PowerShell scripting practices with correct variable assignment, error handling, and logical flow.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The script accurately implements the collection of reconnaissance data from a paid technical database (BuiltWith) via an API, which is a textbook example of T1597.
